title: I validated a startup idea with AI before writing a single line of code
post:
Six weeks. Zero code. $2,000 in AI costs. And I validated that my startup idea had genuine demand.

Here's what I did: used AI to scrape Reddit, analyze competitors, identify customer pain points, draft landing pages, and even run simulated user interviews. The AI generated enough qualitative data to know if people would actually pay.

By the time I started coding, I knew exactly what to build — not what I assumed they needed, but what they'd actually pay for.

This is the new way to validate. Before, you'd spend months building to find out no one wanted it. Now you spend weeks and a few hundred dollars on AI to stress-test the idea first.

Building something people don't want is the most expensive thing in startups. AI makes it optional.

How do you validate ideas? Thoughts?
